reason: Item 08 is a coverage closure on src/simsopt/geo/strain_optimization.py, a Python+JAX-only module with no C++ / simsoptpp dependency. The module-level partial(jit, static_argnames=...) kernels and bare @jit math primitives (Lp_torsion_pure, torstrain_pure, binormstrain_pure) are unchanged. The only edit is wrapping the host gammadash() boundary in a jax.transfer_guard('allow') block plus an as_jax_float64 stage so that the wrapper J/dJ paths run cleanly under SIMSOPT_JAX_TRANSFER_GUARD=disallow. No new production-scale kernel is introduced.

production_scale_floor.ncoils_note: The strain wrappers are per-curve operators. The closest existing production-scale fixture in tests/geo/test_strainopt.py uses NCSX coil 0 alone at coil_order=6, points_per_period=120 (nquadpoints=720); the item 08 closeout test reuses that exact fixture so nquadpoints is well above the per-curve nquadpoints>=64 floor in section 4c.

dense_materialization_budget.gammadash_host_stage: (nquadpoints, 3) float64 NumPy -> JAX array via jax_core._math_utils.as_jax_float64; (720, 3) * 8 bytes = 17280 bytes at the production-scale NCSX fixture; well below max_dense_jacobian_bytes
dense_materialization_budget.lp_strain_penalty_grad_output: (strain shape, gammadash shape) tuple of arrays; both already bounded by the gammadash budget

donation.reason: Wrapper consumes mutable Optimizable state and may be called repeatedly inside scipy.optimize.minimize line searches; donating the gammadash stage buffer would be incorrect.
